channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: How the growing penny shortage may impact you
item.description: Several retailers have started to roll out policies as they run out of pennies. Here's what to expect if you're paying with cash.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Polling places opened Saturday for the start of in-person voting for two of the year's most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor's race and the contest to pick New Jersey's next governor.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Your latest prescription is to get outside
item.description: Health care providers are trying a new tactic to promote the healing effects of nature: They're actually writing prescriptions for it.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Historic libraries bring modern comfort to book lovers and history buffs in New England
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When David Arsenault takes down a worn, leather-bound 19th-century book from the winding shelves of the Boston Athenaeum, he feels a sense of awe — like he’s handling an artifact in a museum.
item.dc:creator: LEAH WILLINGHAM and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 shot near Howard University homecoming week; 2 people in custody
item.description: Five people were hospitalized after a shooting near Howard University Friday, according to the DC Fire and EMS Department.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Dillon, ABC 4

item.title: Trump heads to 3-country sprint in Asia, meeting with Xi as government shutdown drags on at home
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump headed for Asia Friday night for the first time this term, a trip where he’s expected to work on investment deals and peace efforts before meeting face-to-face with Chinese President Xi Jinping to try to deescalate a trade war.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARK SCHIEFELBEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump ends trade talks with Canada over tariffs ad that Ontario premier now says he'll phase out
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced he’s ending “all trade negotiations” with Canada because of a television ad sponsored by one of its provinces that used the words of former President Ronald Reagan to criticize U.S. tariffs — prompting the province's leader to later pull the ad.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, SEUNG MIN KIM and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump speaks at tele-rally to boost GOP's chances in governor's race in New Jersey
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump urged his supporters in New Jersey late Friday to cast a ballot for the Republican candidate for governor, Jack Ciattarelli, before early voting starts in the state on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Canada’s most populous province said Friday he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: Hegseth’s order comes as the Trump administration has been striking alleged drug-smuggling boats in the Caribbean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mafia returns to center stage in NBA betting scandal that included rigged poker games
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Decades after a crackdown by prosecutors decimated the ranks of the New York Mafia, the indictment of an NBA coach, a player and nearly three dozen others in a betting scandal highlighted the mob's persistence and adaptability to changing times and technology.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inflation data unlikely to be released next month, White House says
item.description: The White House says there probably won't be an October inflation report due to the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Poll: California's Prop 50 likely to pass, Republican surges in governor's race
item.description: With less than two weeks before election day, California voters appear poised to pass the state’s redistricting measure, Proposition 50, to counter Republican redistricting efforts in Texas to gain more seats in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: Dashcam video shows deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant trucker
item.description: Federal officials warned California about truck license compliance issues before deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant driver killed three.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands of curling irons recalled amid burn injuries
item.description: Thousands of curling irons sold at popular retailers, including on Amazon, are being recalled after reports of the curling iron barrel detaching, posing a burn risk.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social Security Administration announces 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment
item.description: Social Security and Supplemental Security Income (SSI) benefits will rise 2.8 percent in 2026 after the annual cost-of-living adjustment (COLA), the federal government announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KRON4

item.title: Social Security recipients get a 2.8% cost-of-living boost in 2026
item.description: The Social Security cost-of-living increase will go into effect for Social Security recipients beginning in January.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alaska Airlines operations resume after ground stop over IT outage
item.description: Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it issued a temporary ground stoppage Thursday evening amid an IT outage.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey runaway found in Louisiana. Police make human trafficking arrest
item.description: Detectives from the Vice Unit have also launched an investigation into the human trafficking aspects of the case, resulting in further arrests.
item.dc:creator: Erykah Agers, NewsNation

item.title: Chemists in Ohio warn of rising carfentanil cases as drug mixtures grow more dangerous
item.description: Authorities across the U.S. are warning of carfentanil, a dangerous synthetic opioid 10,000 times more potent than morphine and 100 times stronger than fentanyl.
item.dc:creator: Savannah Young, KRON4

item.title: Alabama executes man with nitrogen gas for 1993 murder over $200 drug debt
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted of helping to burn a man alive in 1993 over a $200 drug debt was executed by nitrogen gas on Thursday. Anthony Boyd, 54, was pronounced dead at 6:33 p.m. at William C. Holman Correctional Facility, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illegal alien failed CDL test 10 times in 2 months before fatal Florida crash that killed 3
item.description: An Illegal alien trucker failed a CDL test 10 times before allegedly causing a crash that killed three people, a senior official with the Florida Attorney General’s Office says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bodycam footage of Jay Cutler's arrest released
item.description: Cutler, 41, was arrested in October 2024 after police responded to a minor traffic collision where he had rear-ended another vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: Deadly semitrailer crash in California renews federal criticism of immigrant truck drivers
item.description: A 21-year-old semitruck driver accused of being under the influence of drugs and causing a fiery crash that killed three people on a southern California freeway is in the country illegally, U.S. Homeland Security officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas connections to illegal gambling investigation, NBA fixing
item.description: An arrest was made in Las Vegas Thursday morning in connection with the FBI investigation of illegal gambling involving current and former NBA coaches and players.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 53 percent disapprove of White House East Wing demolition: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans said in a new poll they disapprove of President Trump’s plans to demolish part of the East Wing in the White House to make room for his $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How inflatable costumes ballooned at anti-Trump rallies due to a protester's frog outfit
item.description: Puffed-out costumes — hippos in tutus, Mr. Potato Heads, dinosaurs — have quickly become a feature of protests against President Donald Trump’s administration, including the massive "No Kings" marches across the U.S. last weekend.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here's where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Broadway musicians reach tentative labor deal, averting a strike
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The union representing Broadway's musicians reached a tentative labor agreement with commercial producers on Thursday, averting a potentially crippling strike that would have silenced nearly two dozen musicals.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: COVID-19 vaccines may help some cancer patients fight tumors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The most widely used COVID-19 vaccines may offer a surprise benefit for some cancer patients – revving up their immune systems to help fight tumors.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minnesota court gives win to transgender powerlifter who was barred from competing as a woman
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — The Minnesota Supreme Court on Wednesday delivered a win to a transgender weightlifter, saying she's protected by the state's expansive human rights law, while the organization that banned her from competing in its women's division said it still has a chance.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI and MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 egg brands recalled for possible salmonella contamination
item.description: A recall issued last week has now expanded to include two brands of eggs that may be contaminated with salmonella.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: Immigration agents conducting sweep on NYC's famed Canal Street confronted by protesters
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An immigration enforcement sweep targeting vendors on Manhattan’s famed Canal Street turned chaotic on Tuesday after droves of angry New Yorkers surrounded federal agents and attempted to block them from driving off, prompting arrests and fierce standoffs along a bustling downtown co
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These are America’s safest – and most dangerous – cities, according to WalletHub
item.description: Threats ranging from public health and natural disasters to financial harm are among the many factors Americans face when they consider moving to a different city.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Man who sent 'So I raped you' message is sentenced to 2 to 4 years for 2013 campus assault
item.description: GETTYSBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man who sent a Facebook message that said, “So I raped you,” to a woman he had sexually assaulted in college in 2013 was sentenced to two to four years in prison on Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: France's former President Sarkozy will begin serving a 5-year prison sentence Tuesday
item.description: Nicolas Sarkozy will become the first former French president in living memory to be imprisoned when he is expected to begin a five-year sentence Tuesday in Paris’ La Santé prison.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY SCHAEFFER and NICOLAS GARRIGA, Associated Press, KTLA 5
